message:
fix: frozen interpreter crash on Linux x86_64

Root cause: CompressedValue's 44-bit pointer compression uses a heap_base
variable to compute pointer offsets. Each frozen shard (733 for TSC) is
compiled as a separate Zig object file. LLVM inlined the heap_base variable
into each shard, constant-folding it to 0, so the arena compression path
was never taken and pointers above 44 bits were silently truncated.

Fix: Move compressed_heap_base to a C global in profile_counters.c (which
is compiled once and linked into the native binary). Frozen shards access
it via extern C functions (edgebox_get/set_compressed_heap_base), ensuring
a single shared instance.

Also: contiguous arena allocator using mmap virtual reservation (8GB) on
Linux to keep all QuickJS heap allocations within a single address range.
